scenarios.title: Semantic Search and Vector Databases
scenarios.when_to_read: Use when a task asks for txtai embeddings, vector search, semantic search, SQL search, hybrid search, graph search, object storage, index save/load, or search troubleshooting.
scenarios.role: Routes agents to txtai-specific embeddings database construction, querying, persistence, and troubleshooting guidance.
scenarios.read_when: txtai, Embeddings, semantic search, vector search, similar(...), SQL search, hybrid search, graph search, subindexes, content=True, FAISS, sparse, dense, object storage
scenarios.best_for: Building and maintaining txtai Embeddings indexes, writing query/filter logic, diagnosing result shapes, and selecting optional ANN/database/graph dependencies.
scenarios.avoid_when: The task is about generic vector databases not using txtai, or about serving an already-built index over FastAPI without changing query logic.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose `txtai` when txtai is the target package and the user's core action is indexing or querying data; route service questions to api-and-deployment after index behavior is clear.

scenarios.title: AI Pipelines, Workflows, RAG, and Agents
scenarios.when_to_read: Use when a task asks for txtai pipelines, workflows, task chains, RAG, LLMs, agents, tool definitions, prompt templates, or multi-step AI orchestration.
scenarios.role: Helps agents choose between deterministic txtai workflows and agentic/LLM/RAG orchestration, then use the correct sub-skill.
scenarios.read_when: txtai Workflow, Task, pipeline, Textractor, Summary, Translation, RAG, LLM, Agent, tools, smolagents, workflow YAML, generator not consumed
scenarios.best_for: Composing task pipelines, debugging lazy workflows, selecting LLM/RAG/agent backends, configuring tools, and producing safe no-download templates.
scenarios.avoid_when: The user only needs a generic LangChain/LlamaIndex answer or asks for a non-txtai orchestration framework.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose pipelines-and-workflows for deterministic batch/stream processing; choose agents-and-llm-orchestration for RAG, model backends, tool choice, and multi-step reasoning.

scenarios.title: Python AI API Services and Deployment
scenarios.when_to_read: Use when a task asks to run txtai as an API, FastAPI service, OpenAI-compatible endpoint, MCP server, secured service, custom route, cluster, console, Docker/cloud/serverless deployment, or observability target.
scenarios.role: Provides txtai Application configuration, route activation, safe writable/reindex boundaries, auth, deployment, and console guidance.
scenarios.read_when: txtai Application, CONFIG=app.yml, uvicorn txtai.api:app, OpenAI-compatible API, /v1/chat/completions, MCP, TOKEN, writable, reindex, Docker, cloud, console --help
scenarios.best_for: Creating service YAML, validating route families, starting Uvicorn, securing writable indexes, deploying containers/cloud patterns, and diagnosing console/API issues.
scenarios.avoid_when: The task only asks for local embeddings query syntax or deterministic workflow internals without service deployment.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose `txtai` when the user wants a running service or configured Application; use sibling sub-skills for the underlying embeddings, workflow, RAG, or agent behavior.
